meta.title: Foundations — Who Will Clean the Toilets?
meta.description: Why talking about task distribution matters before it becomes a conflict. Inspired by Gary Chapman.

book.title: "Profit from the 5 Love Languages"
book.body1: In his book, Gary Chapman — author of The 5 Love Languages — observes that most relationship tension doesn't come from big issues (money, family, intimacy) but from small everyday tasks that were never explicitly assigned. The problem: each person arrives with their unconscious models, talents, preferences, values — and nobody talks about it until it explodes.
book.body2: This tool forces that conversation, in a structured, playful and caring way.

angles.title: Each task is viewed through four angles.
angles.body: Chapman insists: taking on a task isn't just about who knows how. It's also who saw their parents do it, who tolerates it, and who thinks it matters.

domains.title: Six daily friction zones.
domains.body: The questionnaire covers everything that keeps a household running — and that nobody thinks to assign before it becomes a problem.

convergences.label: Your convergences
convergences.detail: topics where you're already aligned, without knowing it.

divergences.label: Your divergences
divergences.detail: areas where a real conversation is needed.

suggestion.label: A suggested distribution
suggestion.detail: based on each person's talents and preferences, not inherited habits.
